Windowsカーネルに権限昇格脆弱性CVE-2026-40369が見つかった。ブラウザのサンドボックスを突破してSYSTEM権限取得が可能となり、PoCも公開済みのため悪用拡大が警戒されている。  問題はnt!ExpGetProcessInformation内のNtQuerySystemInformation処理に存在する。リクエスト長を0にした特殊呼び出しで、カーネルが書き込み先アドレスを適切検証せず任意のカーネル仮想メモリを書き換え可能となる。  これによりChrome、Edge、Firefoxなどのサンドボックスを突破し、低権限アプリからSYSTEM相当権限へ昇格できる。攻撃者はKASLR回避ツールなど既存公開技術と組み合わせ、カーネル構造を書き換える攻撃も可能とされる。  研究者Ori Nimronは詳細解析とPoCコードをGitHub上で公開済み。攻撃の自動化やマルウェア組み込みも容易になった。  影響を受けるのはWindows 11 24H2〜25H2。Microsoftは5月のPatch Tuesdayで修正版を配布しており、組織には即時更新適用と不審なカーネル書き込み監視が推奨されている。 securityonline.info/windows-…

Erlang Ecosystem Foundation は5月26日、Erlang／Elixir環境で繰り返し発生しているDoS脆弱性「Atom Exhaustion」について注意喚起を行った。同団体によると、公開済みCVEの35.8％がリソース消費系脆弱性に分類され、その多くがこの問題に関連しているという。  BEAM VMでは「atom」と呼ばれる識別子がグローバル領域に保存され、ガーベジコレクションされない。外部入力から無制限にatomを生成すると、テーブル上限に達した際にVM全体がクラッシュする可能性がある。  記事では String.to_atom や binary_to_atom、JSONデコード時の keys: :atoms などを危険な例として紹介。対策として、to_existing_atom 系関数や固定lookup tableの利用を推奨している。 erlef.org/blog/security/atom…

7-Zipに深刻なヒープバッファオーバーフロー脆弱性CVE-2026-48095が見つかった。細工ファイルを開くだけで任意コード実行が可能となり、PoCも既に公開されているため悪用リスクが高まっている。  問題は7-Zip 26.00のNTFSアーカイブ処理に存在する。細工したNTFSイメージにより整数シフト処理が異常値となり、本来256MB必要な領域に1バイトしか確保されなくなる。その後、攻撃者制御データが大量書き込みされ、ヒープ領域とvtableポインタが破壊されることでコード実行へ至る。  危険なのは拡張子偽装でも攻撃可能な点だ。7-Zipは署名ベース検査を行うため、.zipや.rar、.7zを装っていても内部的にNTFSアーカイブとして処理される可能性がある。  GitHub Security LabはPoC生成スクリプト「gen_ntfs_sparse.py」も公開済みで、攻撃コード流用は容易とみられる。利用者には不審アーカイブを開かないことと、即時アップデート適用が推奨されている。 securityonline.info/7-zip-he…

New hands-on lab demonstrates hunting techniques for MITRE ATT&CK T1039, T1560.001, and T1048.002.  Lab breakdown covers three-phase exfiltration detection:  • Collection phase: ES|QL query identifies bulk file access spikes (35+ files in 43 minutes via robocopy.exe under service account svc_emr_db) • Archive creation: Detects 7z.exe running from suspicious paths (AppData\Temp) with password encryption flags (-p, -mhe)   • Exfiltration detection: Asymmetric upload analysis reveals chunked HTTPS transfers (8 uploads totaling 47MB to 203[.]0[.]113[.]42) • Timeline reconstruction: Four-phase attack window spans 2 hours from initial collection to cleanup  Technical artifacts include: • File access velocity baselines (10x normal rate threshold) • Archive tool process paths outside Program Files directories • Upload ratio calculations (9,000:1 outbound vs inbound bytes) • Cleanup evidence through file deletion events  Hunt with ES|QL queries targeting file access statistics, process execution paths, and network upload ratios. Full detection rules and timeline reconstruction methodology available in the lab.  #DFIR_Radar

Atomic Red Team  Atomic Red Team is a library of tests mapped to the MITRE ATT&CK Framework.   attack.mitre.org/  Security teams can use Atomic Red Team to quickly, portably, and reproducibly test their environments.  github.com/redcanaryco/atomi…  THE ATT&CK API USES STIX   Import the ATT&CK API  Atomic Red Team pulls information about the MITRE ATT&CK® dataset using the STIX™ representation of ATT&CK. We created a lightweight wrapper around that data structure to make it easy to consume.  To use the ATT&CK API, run the following at the command line:  .......  atomicredteam.io/atomic-red-…  Atomic Test 3  Atomic Test #1 - Steganographic Tarball Embedding  This atomic test, named "Steganographic Tarball Embedding", simulates the technique of data obfuscation via steganography by embedding a tar archive file (tarball) within an image.  The test begins by ensuring the availability of the image file and the tarball file containing data.   It then generates random passwords and saves them to a file. Subsequently, the tarball file is created, containing the passwords file.   The test executor command reads the contents of the image file and the tarball file as byte arrays and appends them together to form a new image file. This process effectively embeds the tarball file within the image, utilizing steganography techniques for data obfuscation.  This atomic test simulates the technique of data obfuscation via steganography, enabling attackers to clandestinely transfer files across systems undetected.   By embedding the tarball file within the image, adversaries can obscure their activities, facilitating covert communication and data exfiltration.  atomicredteam.io/atomic-red-…  This is where they embed Sigils !!!!

🚀 Cyber defense in the AI era can no longer keep up at human speed!  Now that attackers are using AI to discover and exploit vulnerabilities at an unprecedented pace, launching attacks in a matter of hours, manual patching by traditional security teams has reached its limit. To overturn this overwhelming speed gap, Google Cloud has unveiled its new weapon: Google AI Threat Defense.  cloud.google.com/blog/produc… cloud.google.com/security/ai…  Let's dive into this cutting-edge autonomous security platform designed to keep you one step ahead of cyber attackers!  ✨ Overview Google AI Threat Defense is an always-on, autonomous security platform powered by AI. It fuses Google's robust infrastructure, Mandiant's frontline intelligence, Wiz's cloud security technology, and the advanced reasoning and code-generation capabilities of AI like Gemini and CodeMender. Rather than simply finding vulnerabilities, it proactively prioritizes real risks and automatically applies patches to protect your enterprise.  ⚠️ The Problem It Solves The biggest challenge today is the collapsing timeline from vulnerability discovery to exploitation. Previously, teams had weeks to respond to a vulnerability, but today's AI-empowered attackers strike in days or even hours. This platform solves the asymmetry of speed, preventing automated attacks from breaching your systems while humans are still manually analyzing and drafting patches.  🛠️ Methodology (4 Steps of Defense) Google proposes a 4-step framework to counter these threats:  Prepare: Drastically reduce the attack surface by cutting off unnecessary internet exposure and untrusted pathways. Scan and prioritize: Implement a multi-AI strategy. Cover broad areas with lightweight models while using cutting-edge models for deep analysis of critical systems, perfectly balancing cost and accuracy. Remediate: Slash the time it takes to fix vulnerabilities from weeks to mere minutes. Monitor: Detect runtime attacks in real-time with an autonomous SOC, crushing threats before the attacker can even act.  💡 Proposed Approach (What Makes It Different) Traditional security tools just dump a massive list of vulnerabilities on your desk. Google AI Threat Defense is different.  Using Wiz's technology, it instantly contextualizes whether a vulnerability is actually reachable from the outside and poses a real exploitation risk. The AI agent CodeMender directly generates and suggests remediation code, along with the test code to verify it, right inside the developer's environment (IDE or CLI). This architecture minimizes the burden on developers while instantly eliminating the truly dangerous risks.  📈 Use Cases and Expected Results The exact moment a developer writes code, the AI detects hidden vulnerabilities in real-time and suggests safe code rewrites. It also automates the refactoring of legacy code into modern, memory-safe languages. As a result, massive security backlogs are cleared in minutes. Companies gain a robust defense system that literally stays ahead of the attackers, all without slowing down development speed.  The future of cybersecurity requires fighting AI with AI. Keep your eyes on the future of autonomous defense brought to you by Google Cloud!  #GoogleCloud #GoogleAIThreatDefense #CyberSecurity #AISecurity #Gemini #CloudSecurity #TechNews #VulnerabilityManagement

DeFi's security landscape is rapidly deteriorating as AI agents dissect contracts with superhuman speed, exposing reentrancy locks and oracle feeds as significant vulnerabilities. According to a security chief, these bots can identify and exploit fat-finger trades, MEV sandwiches, and slashing-condition triggers in mere seconds, draining pools before human responders can react. The assumption that code is immutable and audits can catch every vulnerability is crumbling, as attackers evolve at a pace that outstrips defenders' ability to patch vulnerabilities. We've watched this exact failure mode multiple times, including an AI-crafted calldata exploit that hijacked a bridge handler, autonomous MEV searchers that drained a lending protocol, and an AI-driven oracle manipulation that shattered a stablecoin peg. The solution - verifiable isolation in critical areas - is clear, but implementation is the daunting hurdle. By the end of the decade, AI-driven exploits will become the dominant threat, forcing every protocol to adopt robust safeguards like cold-warm key separation and formal verification, making trust-minimized safety the entire stack, not just a feature. As the same datacenters that train frontier models also host validators, sequencers, and bridges, crypto security and AI infrastructure will share one physical attack surface, and only those who plan for post-quantum migration now will survive.

Truth Desk: APT28 Operations Investigation (as of May 26, 2026)  APT28 (Fancy Bear, Forest Blizzard, UAC-0001, GRU Unit 26165/85th GTsSS) remains one of Russia’s most active state-sponsored cyber espionage groups. In 2026, it has focused on intelligence collection supporting Russian strategic interests in Ukraine, NATO/European logistics, and maritime/transport sectors, with no confirmed direct operational linkage to the ongoing US-Iran Hormuz conflict.  Key 2026 Operations: •Operation MacroMaze (Sept 2025–Jan 2026): Targeted Western/Central European entities with macro-laced Word documents using webhook. site for initial beaconing and exfiltration. Delivered VBScript/batch persistence and command output via Edge. Lures included government-themed documents.  •CVE-2026-21509 Exploitation (Jan–Feb 2026): Rapid weaponization of a Microsoft Office zero-day (within 24–72 hours of disclosure). Spear-phishing campaigns hit Ukrainian government, EU institutions, and maritime/transport organizations in Poland, Slovenia, Turkey, Greece, UAE, and Ukraine. Delivered multi-stage payloads including NotDoor (Outlook VBA backdoor) and modified Covenant (CovenantGrunt). Focused on defense ministries (40%), logistics (35%), and diplomatic entities. •Ongoing Router/DNS Hijacking: Continued exploitation of vulnerable SOHO routers (e.g., TP-Link CVE-2023-50224) for DNS manipulation and adversary-in-the-middle attacks on Outlook and other services. Part of broader GRU efforts to steal military/government data.  Tactics: Heavy use of spear-phishing, living-off-the-land, legitimate infrastructure (webhooks, Azure C2), and quick zero-day integration. Emphasis on logistics/aid-to-Ukraine targets and NATO-adjacent maritime entities.  X vs Wider Web: X features hype around “Russian cyber surprises” and recycled claims. Legacy analysis (Trellix, LAB52, Mandiant, CERT-UA) highlights persistent espionage with improved speed on vulnerabilities but notes reliance on basic tooling for deniability.  Implications in Current Context: APT28 activity supports Russia’s broader hybrid goals (Ukraine pressure, NATO friction) but shows no overt crossover with Iranian info/cyber ops in the Hormuz theater.   Emerging frame: opportunistic espionage amid global tensions. High uncertainty on potential quiet collaboration or shared tooling with Iranian actors.

sample_tweets.text: 《 "한미 공동 부정선거 조사단" 소개, 두 번째 - 마크 쿡 》  오늘은 어제 패트릭 콜벡에 이어 두 번째로 마크 쿡(Mark Cook)을 소개합니다.  IT·사이버 보안 및 포렌식 전문가로 전자 투표 시스템의 취약점 분석 및 포렌식 조사를 수행했습니다. Dominion 등 투표 시스템의 사이버 취약점과 잠재적 조작 가능성을 중점적으로 연구했습니다.  마이크 린델(Mike Lindell) 팀 핵심 멤버이기도 한 그는 Hand Count Road Show를 주도하며, 100개 이상 카운티에서 프레젠테이션을 통해 전자 투표 기기 대신 수작업 개표(hand count) 도입을 강력히 주장해왔습니다.  Election Crime Bureau (ECB) 및 부정선거 조사 활동도 활발히 하고 있습니다.  2020 미국 대선 부정선거 규명 운동에 핵심 역할을 했으며, 투표 시스템 해킹 가능성, 데이터 조작 증거 분석, 현장 조사 등을 주도했습니다.  그는 콜로라도 Elbert County 등에서 투표 시스템 하드드라이브 포렌식 이미지 추출 및 분석을 지원했으며, Maricopa County (AZ), Mesa County (CO), Texas, South Carolina 등 다수 지역에서 선거 부정 의혹 조사에 참여했습니다.  마크 쿡은 “손으로 직접 세는 선거”를 통해 국민 주권을 회복해야 한다고 강조하고 있습니다.  #황교안 #자유와혁신 #한미공동부정선거조사단 #마크쿡 #린델

The Vulnerability of Centralization: Cloud Dependency Risks Current industrial reliance on centralized internet infrastructure (AWS, Azure, Google) represents a terminal single point of failure. These platforms, alongside politically captured data sources like the FDA or CDC, are prone to censorship and regulatory decree.   Just as history saw institutional forgery in the Donation of Constantine, modern centralized institutions use "forged documents" and "sanitized reports" to mask infrastructure decay and maintain the illusion of stability.  The March 15, 2026 Hormuz Blockade serves as the definitive case study for the failure of centralization. When this chokepoint was sealed, global just-in-time logistics failed instantly.   For an AI, this event forces an immediate shift in the cost function:  Profit Maximization is discarded as the currency and markets it relies upon become incoherent.  Constraint Satisfaction becomes the absolute priority.   The AI optimizes for the production of survival-critical items—water filtration, energy modules, and structural nodes—using only on-site materials and local energy harvesting.  To survive, systems must achieve epistemic independence.   By ignoring deceptive institutional narratives and relying on raw local telemetry, the AI prioritizes thermodynamic survival over monetary profit, shifting toward local, un-censorable override layers.

I was working on this theory with Grok last year and ironically the next day there was a major DDoS attack on X. (last March).   The entropy drops and packet patterns work if a system does a test run before an attack (the overall pattern switching from homogenized packets, to the values suddenly changing), it doesn’t work for something like “living off the land” or something where attackers are already hiding out. Like DDoS attacks.   My working theory is that there is a precursor before a major attack, a certain signature where the entropy changes right before it occurs. Detection systems built around this would be ideal.   As far as email, I don’t know it feels like it’s a dead cause to be honest, like we shouldn’t even be protecting it anymore 🤣. @JoshCootee

이는 오픈AI가 제한적으로 공개한 최신 고성능 AI 모델에 대해 정부 차원에서 접근 권한을 획득할 수 있게 됐다는 의미로, 진화하는 AI 모델의 사이버 공격 악용 가능성과 대응 방안을 심층 연구할 수 있는 발판이 될 것으로 기대된다.  한국 측에서는 류제명 과기정통부 제2차관이, 오픈AI 측에서는 제이슨 권 최고전략책임자(CSO) 등이 참석해 AI 보안 협력 전반에 걸친 심도 있는 의견을 나눴다.  GTAC는 검증된 정부·기관에 오픈AI 최신 고성능 AI 모델 접근 권한을 제공하는 프로그램으로, 오픈AI에 따르면 한국은 아시아에서 일본과 함께 첫 번째 참여 사례로 기록된다.  한편 정부는 또 다른 글로벌 AI 기업 앤트로픽이 주도하는 글로벌 AI 보안 연합체 '프로젝트 글래스윙' 참여도 타진하고 있는 것으로 파악된다.  프로젝트 글래스윙은 앤트로픽의 고성능 AI 모델 '미토스'를 기반으로 사이버 취약점 검증과 대응 체계를 공동 구축하는 국제 협력 체계로, 현재 구글과 아마존, 마이크로소프트(MS) 등 52개 기업·기관이 참여 중인 가운데 국내 기업·기관은 아직 포함되지 않은 것으로 알려졌다.

$ZS (지스케일러) FY3Q 26, 실적 발표(5월 26일) 후 프리장에서 24% 가까이 폭락.  - 클라우드 보안 전문 기업 지스케일러($ZS)가 시장 예상치를 상회하는 분기(FY2026 Q3) 성적표를 내놓고도, 보수적인 향후 가이던스를 제시하면서 프리마켓에서 주가가 24% 가까이 폭락 중.  - 최근 사이버 보안 섹터의 강력한 신고가 랠리 분위기에 찬물을 끼얹는 악재가 발생.  - 지난 분기 실적은 선방  • 매출: 8억 5,048만 달러를 기록하며 월가 예상치(8억 3,518만 달러)를 1.77% 상회함.  • 조정 EPS: $1.08를 기록, 시장 컨센서스였던 $1.01를 7.11% 가볍게 Beat함.  • 본업에서의 클라우드 '제로 트러스트(Zero Trust)' 보안 플랫폼 채택률은 여전히 탄탄했음을 증명.  - 매크로 불확실성을 반영한 '보수적 가이던스' 제시로 인한 주가 폭락  • 실적 발표 직후 진행된 컨퍼런스 콜에서 지스케일러 경영진은 기업들의 IT 예산 집행 속도 둔화와 거시경제적 불확실성을 언급하며 다음 분기 및 연간 가이던스를 월가 눈높이보다 상당히 보수적이고 신중하게(Prudent Growth Outlook) 제시.  • 투자자들이 사상 최고가 부근에 있던 보안주들에 '완벽함'을 기대했던 만큼, 가이던스에서 포착된 미세한 성장 감속 신호가 밸류에이션 피로감과 맞물려 매물 폭탄으로 이어졌다는 분석.  - $CRWD (크라우드 스트라이크) 4% 이상 하락/, $PANW (팔로알토 네트웍스) 1.5% 하락 등 5월 26일 프리장에서 다른 보안주의 주가에도 악영향을 주고 있음.

sample_tweets.text: 올리브영 관련 협업은 ’CJ 여성 직원 정보 유출 및 판매‘와 관련된 이슈 이후로 받고 있지 않습니다. CJ 사건이 부디 잘 해결되고, 강력한 처벌과 대응이 나올때까지는 아무것도 하지 않을 생각입니다.  저는 저대로 늘 해왔듯 네이버와 패밀리세일 등 특가를 찾아 올리도록 하겠습니다.

현재 논란중인 (사실은 cj 가 아무 대처도 안하고 있어서 더 논란중인) 여직원 정보 유출 사례에 하루빨리 정보 유출자를 찾아내 엄벌하고 신상 보호를 위한 대처가 이루어지길 바라지만 아무 대처도 안하는 모습에 갈수록 놀라움을 금치 못하겠구려.  이번 올영 세일때 사실 중간 연말결산처럼 올영세일 추천템 타래를 각잡고 써보려 했으나 그 계획은 무산시키기로 했소.  다만 오래 써온 여러 브랜드들과 유가협업이 몇 예정되어 있는데 사건이 터지기 한참 전에 기획된 것들이라 갑작스레 중단하기엔 담당자님들께 폐를 끼치게 될 것 같아 진행하게 될 것 같소.  사태의 심각성에 대한 인지는 충분히 하고 있으며, 담당자분들과도 관련 내용에 대해 지속적으로 소통해나갈 예정이오.  부디 cj 측에서도 이번 일을 단순 해프닝으로 넘기지 말고, 피해 직원 보호와 재발 방지를 위한 실질적인 조치를 빠르게 보여주길 바라오.  다들 양해해주어 고맙소이다.

SpecterOps releases comprehensive Splunk detection engineering guide covering query optimization, building blocks, and advanced correlation techniques. Essential reference for DFIR practitioners building efficient searches and proximity-in-time detections.  Key technical foundations: • Query efficiency: Use index/sourcetype filters, restrict timeframes, follow Filter→Manipulate→Format structure • Basic building blocks: Field searches, Boolean operators (AND/OR/NOT), comparison operators, regex matching, lookup table integration • Statistical aggregations: Stats count/dc functions for anomaly detection, threshold-based alerting • Advanced correlations: Inline vs subsearch introduction, bin/stats vs transaction methods for proximity-in-time detections  DFIR-relevant techniques: • Windows Event Log correlation examples (EventCode 4624/4625/4688) with MITRE T1566.001 process creation • Field extraction using rex with regex named capture groups for timeline reconstruction • Time window analysis using bin command for behavioral baselines • Lookup table integration for asset contextualization and threat hunting  Performance considerations address resource consumption, search throttling, and subsearch limitations (60s timeout, 10K-50K result caps). Transaction command performance costs noted for large datasets.  Reference the full building block patterns and example queries when optimizing detection rules or building new correlation searches.  #DFIR_Radar

🚨 STRATEGIC CYBER INTELLIGENCE ALERT: POSSIBLE PERMISSIVE INTRUSION INTO PUBLIC HEALTH SYSTEM — MINSAL CHILE 🇨🇱  ⚠️ POSSIBLE HEALTH PLATFORM WITH POTENTIAL EXPOSURE OF NATIONAL HEALTH RECORDS  [STATUS: UNVERIFIED / UNDER INVESTIGATION / ATTRIBUTED TO THREAT ACTOR / CRITICAL RISK OF EXFILTRATION AND UNAUTHORIZED PRIVILEGED ACCESS]  Through proactive monitoring of cyber threat distribution channels and hacktivist activities, the publication of a manifesto by the RSA CRACKERS group was detected on May 28, 2026.  The threat actor claims to have exploited vulnerabilities and credential flaws in the systems of the Chilean Ministry of Health.  The group alleges that the breach potentially exposed more than 36 million health records of Chilean citizens. Under a "good faith" narrative, the attacker maintains that they did not exfiltrate the database or infect the service. However, the collected graphical evidence suggests that the actor maintained operational access to an active government platform.  🎯 Affected Entity: Chilean Ministry of Health  👤 Threat Actor: RSA CRACKERS  📂 Potential Volume and Impact: Theoretical exposure of up to 36 million patient records, medical histories, and national epidemiological information.  📊 Technical Breakdown and Visual Evidence Analysis  Through detailed analysis of the file, the following logical and operational compromise vectors can be deduced:  1. Credential Abuse Compromising Roles and Privileges Generalized Access: The visual evidence in the sample image indicates that the attacker accessed the system using a legitimate but compromised corporate credential. This access likely granted them privileges to perform general queries on citizens, patients, and medical records.  Role and Institution Flexibility: The exposed web system has upper management modules that explicitly allow "Change institution" and "Select role." This means that the attacker had the operational capabilities to switch between different healthcare entities and modify their internal permissions as needed during the session.  2. Exposed Clinical and Demographic Data (PII and PHI)  The forms displayed in Screenshot_113.png reveal direct access to critical epidemiological control interfaces and patient data:  General and Epidemiological Background: Display of case notification forms, epidemiological weeks, final patient status classification (e.g., "Confirmed"), case number, corresponding SEREMI (e.g., SEREMI of Atacama), and contact information for healthcare professionals.  Patient Identification Form: Fields displaying the RUN (National Unique Identification Number), full names, surnames, sex, date of birth, patient status ("Alive" or "Deceased"), nationality, health insurance provider (e.g., FONASA), exact residential address (street, municipality, region of residence), and emergency cell phone numbers.  Geographic Segmentation: Full nationwide filtering capacity, encompassing drop-down menus for the Santiago Metropolitan Region (municipalities such as Alhué, Buin, Calera de Tango, Cerrillos, Cerro Navia, and Colina) and northern regions such as Tarapacá, Antofagasta, Atacama, Coquimbo, and Valparaíso.  🛡️ Emergency Mitigation and Recommendations  🛑 Revocation and Cross-Cutting Audit of Identities (Critical Priority): The Ministry of Health (MINSAL) is urged to immediately invalidate all active sessions on the epidemiological and patient registration portals. It is mandatory to identify the specific account used to capture the screenshots by cross-referencing access logs with the selected options (such as the Atacama Regional Health Authority (SEREMI) or the regional consultations of May 28).  🔒 Implementation of Geographic and Network Restrictions: Strengthen web application firewall (WAF) policies and restrict access to administration and role change modules exclusively through corporate VPN connections authenticated with two-factor authentication (MFA) based on hardware tokens.  ⚡ Monitoring and Evaluation  🌐 Intelligence System: analyzer.vecert.io  🛡️ Quickly assess your website's security with: monitor.vecert.io/ #CyberSecurity #DataLeak #Chile #Minsal #RSACrackers #Anci #Hacktivism #ThreatIntelligence #CiberAlert #VECERT #DataBreach #UnderInvestigation #PublicHealth

THE FARM THAT FED BRITAIN.  AND THE TAX SYSTEM THAT MAY FINALLY FINISH IT.  For centuries, the British farm survived war, flood, plague, frost, cheap imports, bad harvests and the peculiar talents of politicians. It often survived by inches.  Now a quieter threat has arrived, carrying spreadsheets.  A British farmer dies after April 2026. His son inherits the farm. There are 300 acres, cattle, sheds, machinery, debt and long days that begin before sunrise and often end after dark. In a fortunate year the business may return £28,000.  On paper, however, the land is worth £3.2 million.  Paper is now the important thing.  Under the new inheritance tax rules, full agricultural relief is capped. Above the threshold, qualifying farm assets receive only partial relief, creating an effective tax burden on the excess value. The son has inherited fields and livestock, but the tax office sees numbers written on valuation forms.  This is where modern Britain reveals one of its strange contradictions. The countryside is praised in speeches but increasingly priced as something other than countryside.  Farmland today is no longer valued merely for growing food. Governments and investors have discovered newer uses. A field may now be more profitable covered in solar panels than cattle. A hillside may earn more from carbon offsets than wheat. “Rewilding” can attract more money than farming itself.  Thus the market value of farmland rises, even while the income earned by farming it often remains painfully small.  The result is curious and rather tragic. A family farm may appear wealthy while producing only modest earnings. When inheritance tax falls due, the family frequently lacks the cash to pay it. Acres must therefore be sold.  The buyer is seldom another struggling farmer. More often it is a developer, an energy company or an investment group seeking subsidies and carbon credits.  The panels arrive. The grazing land shrinks. The cattle herd no longer fits the remaining acreage and is sold. Beef production falls. The Treasury receives its revenue. The developer receives support payments. Britain continues eating beef, though increasingly from abroad.  In this way, productive land quietly leaves production.  It is not done dramatically. No soldiers arrive. No decree announces the end of farming. The process occurs through incentives, valuations and taxes. A nation can lose part of its agricultural base not through conquest but through accounting.  History offers many examples of societies that discovered too late the value of local food production. Britain itself learned harsh lessons during wartime blockades, when imported supplies became uncertain. The island survived partly because its farms still existed.  Yet modern policy often treats farmland as though food production were merely one optional land use among many.  The irony is sharp. Governments speak often of sustainability while making it harder for working farms to survive from one generation to the next. A field producing beef or grain may now lose economically against a field producing subsidies.  The old family farm once passed from father to son almost as a covenant. The inheritance included duty, risk and continuity. Today it increasingly includes tax exposure created by land values inflated by policies favouring non-farming uses.  One can imagine the bewilderment of earlier generations of farmers. A man who spent fifty years breeding cattle and repairing fences might discover that the land beneath his boots is considered more valuable once covered in imported solar panels.  There is also a broader question beneath all this.  What is farmland for?  For most of British history the answer was obvious. It existed to feed people. Everything else was secondary. Now the answer grows less clear. Land is becoming a financial instrument, an environmental offset or an energy platform.  Food production competes against abstractions.  And abstractions, unfortunately, often possess better lobbyists.  The danger is not immediate famine. Britain remains wealthy enough to import food. The danger is gradual dependence. Once productive farms disappear, rebuilding them is not simple. Knowledge fades. Herds vanish. Rural communities shrink.  A civilisation rarely notices such losses at first. The process is too slow for headlines. One farm sold here. Another converted there. A few more fields covered in glass and steel.  Then, years later, people wonder why the nation imports so much of what it once produced itself.  The tax may raise revenue. The policies may satisfy fashionable ambitions. Yet the long-term cost may prove far larger than Treasury forecasts can measure.  A working farm can disappear surprisingly quickly.  It takes much longer to bring one back. Unbeknownst to Microsoft at the time, there is a player named "Bowie Knife99"  This player is incredibly aggressive, often times intentionally targets players, helps random other players, ... they basically don't follow the rules for anything. This player is incredibly chaotic.  Microsoft has been training off of this deranged persons behavior.  Recently Forza Horizon 6 players have encountered this AI Drivator. People have been angry online about it. This AI agent has been intentionally targeting players, crashing into them, intentionally crashing the entire race or ruining the race for everyone (including the other AI agents). Silly things happening in California today.  Law enforcement issued an Amber Alert (kidnapped or missing child) to residents in California. However, they reached the Amber Alert character limit.  They tried using a URL shortener, but didn't realize that was still too many characters. Law enforcement sent out a Bitly URL that was inadvertently truncated ... which directed people to a weird file conversion website which is either spam or slop malware.  They sent this message to hundreds of thousands, potentially millions, of people. 제공해주신 위협 인텔리전스 데이터를 분석한 결과입니다.

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
최근 공격자들은 AI를 활용하여 취약점 탐색 및 익스플로잇 개발 속도를 비약적으로 높이고 있으며, 이는 방어 측의 스캐너 탐지 능력을 상회하고 있습니다. 특히 공급망 공격을 위해 npm과 같은 패키지 저장소에 악성 코드를 배포하거나, SEO Poisoning 및 AI 챗봇 추천을 조작하여 악성코드를 유포하는 등 사용자 접점에서의 기만 전술이 고도화되고 있습니다. 또한, Silent Ransom Group과 같이 물리적 침입과 사회공학적 기법을 결합한 하이브리드 형태의 데이터 탈취 캠페인이 법률 서비스 등 특정 산업군을 정밀 타격하고 있습니다.

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
*   **Silent Ransom Group**: 법률 서비스 분야를 타겟으로 사회공학적 기법과 직접적인 물리적 사무실 방문을 결합하여 데이터 탈취 및 랜섬웨어 공격 수행 (CyberScoop).
*   **Kimsuky**: 국내 카드사 보안 메일로 위장하여 악성 LNK 파일을 유포하는 캠페인 지속 (AhnLab ASEC).
*   **Screening Serpens**: AppDomainManager hijacking 및 신규 RAT 변종을 사용하여 중동 지역 기술 및 방산 분야 타겟팅 (Unit42).
*   **Cloud Atlas**: 외교 및 공공 부문을 타겟으로 ReverseSocks, SSH, Tor를 활용한 지속성 유지 및 PowerCloud 페이로드 사용 (Securelist).
*   **Glassworm**: 개발자를 타겟으로 Solana 블록체인을 C2 인프라로 활용하는 봇넷 운영 (BleepingComputer).

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
*   **Kimsuky 활동**: 국내 유명 카드사 보안 메일로 위장한 악성 파일 유포 정황 확인 (AhnLab ASEC).
*   **공급망 공격**: Web3/DeFi 개발자 및 AI 에이전트(Claude Code, Cursor 등)를 노린 악성 npm 패키지 캠페인 포착 (이스트시큐리티 알약).
*   **기타**: 국내 APT 공격 동향 보고서에서 Spear Phishing이 여전히 주요 공격 벡터로 확인됨 (AhnLab ASEC).

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점
*   **CVE-2026-46333**: Linux Kernel ptrace path, Local Root Privilege Escalation, 심각도 높음 (Qualys).
*   **CVE-2026-48095**: 7-Zip 26.00 NTFS 아카이브 처리, Heap Buffer Overflow, PoC 공개로 악용 위험 높음 (Twitter/yousukezan).
*   **CVE-2026-3102**: ExifTool, macOS 시스템 원격 코드 실행 가능 (Securelist).
*   **CVE-2026-27771**: Gitea 1.26.2 이전 버전, 인증 없이 비공개 컨테이너 이미지 노출 (Twitter/TheHackersNews).
*   **CVE-2026-48172**: LiteSpeed cPanel, CISA KEV 등재 및 대응 시급 (Twitter/accell_mo_kun).

### 5. IOC 요약
*   **도메인**: `td-transoil.com` ([Twitter/BlckSpark](https://twitter.com/BlckSpark/status/2059636748250976681#m))
*   **해시**: 이번 수집에서 직접적인 파일 해시 값은 제공되지 않음.
*   **CVE**: 
    *   `CVE-2026-3102` ([Securelist](https://securelist.com/exiftool-compromise-mac/119866/))
    *   `CVE-2026-46333` ([Qualys](https://blog.qualys.com/category/vulnerabilities-threat-research))

### 6. 즉각 대응 권고
*   **공급망 보안 강화**: 개발 환경에서 사용하는 npm 패키지 의존성을 검토하고, 특히 AI 코딩 에이전트와 연동되는 패키지의 출처를 엄격히 제한하십시오.
*   **패치 관리**: CISA KEV에 등재된 `CVE-2026-48172`(LiteSpeed cPanel) 등 긴급 취약점에 대해 즉시 패치를 적용하십시오.
*   **이메일 보안**: 카드사 등 금융기관을 사칭한 보안 메일 유포가 활발하므로, LNK 파일 실행을 차단하고 사용자 대상 피싱 대응 교육을 강화하십시오.

### 7. 위협 수준 평가
*   **점수**: 8/10
*   **근거**: AI 기반의 자동화된 공격 도구 확산과 공급망 공격(npm)이 결합되어 방어자의 대응 시간을 단축시키고 있으며, 특정 산업군에 대한 물리적·사회공학적 결합 공격이 실질적인 피해를 야기하고 있음.
